rulings.Case Title: JACK LAPIDOS VS. DIANDRE NORWOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2025. Line 1. PLAINTIFF JACK LAPIDOS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: REARDON HEIGHTS ASSOCIATES, L.P. VS. ALVIN WILLIAMS JR. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2025. Line 3.1. DEFENDANT ALVIN WILLIAMS MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Paragraphs 15 and 19d are stricken from the Complaint as improper. Even broadly construing the allegations on the face of the Complaint, the only reasonable inference that may be drawn is that there is no written agreement between the parties to this action, much less one that specifically provides for attorney fees. Plaintiff cites no legal authority for the proposition that it may hypothetically be entitled to attorney fees based upon a written agreement to which Plaintiff is not a party, which may or may not exist. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: REARDON HEIGHTS ASSOCIATES, L.P. VS. ALVIN WILLIAMS JR. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2025. Line 3.2. DEFENDANT ALVIN WILLIAMS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, with leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Specifically, the demurer is sustained with leave to amend only as to the objections to the written rental agreement attached to the Complaint as Exhibit 1. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to attach the appropriate rental agreement and revise the allegations regarding the rental agreement accordingly. The demurrer is otherwise overruled.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
